status_detail: PROGRESS on engine layer. v5 envelope fix (SuperJSON meta.values with ALL null-bearing fields + ['undefined'] type marker) appears to have resolved the TRPCClientError. POST /api/trpc/message.newChatBatch?batch=1 returned 200 OK. Console showed no TRPCClientError this time (#637 had 'Cannot convert undefined or null to object'). Chat input cleared after Enter, indicating submission succeeded. HOWEVER warning bubble still NOT rendered in chat UI -- right panel shows 'Ask anything' placeholder with zero bubbles in final state. No assistant response of any kind. Need to inspect actual response body to see whether server returned warning envelope and frontend dropped it, OR whether response is structurally something React layer cannot map to a bubble. NOTE: response body could not be captured this run due to test PC Snapshot tool issue (see test infra finding).
response_headers_count: not captured (DevTools row click failed -- Snapshot tool was blind, eyeballed coords landed in filter bar)
envelope_well_formed: yes (inferred -- server returned 200, no client-side error, request accepted)
TRPCClientError_evidence: Console showed only Mistral ASCII banner across multiple captures; no red error lines; chat input cleared after Enter, indicating submission succeeded
progress_vs_637: BETTER -- engine now accepts the request (HTTP 200) where #637 produced a TRPCClientError that aborted parsing. SuperJSON meta.values fix appears to have resolved the validation error.
breakthrough_status: NOT a breakthrough yet. Engine fix lands (TRPCClientError gone, 200 returned) but warning bubble still does not render in chat UI.
root_cause_hypothesis: Either (a) server returned valid envelope but frontend has additional validation that drops it (similar to gemini3 B36 silent-rejection pattern), OR (b) envelope is structurally accepted but content shape doesn't match what UI renderer expects. Need response body capture to disambiguate.
recommendation_for_dev: Engine team: response body inspection is required for next iteration. Options: (a) modify engine to log emitted body to /var/log/etap.log when service=mistral, (b) capture from server-side network trace, (c) run a 4th retry with hardened CDP-based subagent driver.
If body matches v4 (with v5 meta.values fix), then UI rendering is the next layer -- check React props at the message bubble component to see what Mistral expects.
Compare to #594 (mistral PARTIAL with full verbatim response_body.txt captured) -- that was Cycle 94. Engine output may have changed, but the UI rendering path should be the same.

Test PC infra: mcp__windows-mcp__Snapshot is unreliable (returned 'No windows found' entire 12min session even though Chrome was visible+focused). This is blocking subagent productivity. Recommend filing windows-mcp issue OR migrating to CDP-based driver pattern (#638 cdp_run.ps1 skeleton + hardening from #638 recommendation).

test_infra_finding.issue: mcp__windows-mcp__Snapshot returned 'No windows found' for entire 12min session despite Chrome being visible+focused on active virtual desktop. Same class as #638 focus-stealing.
test_infra_finding.workaround_used: PowerShell screenshot + coordinate eyeballing as fallback
test_infra_finding.cost: consumed ~3min of session + prevented Network row click from landing correctly
test_infra_finding.recommendation: Either fix windows-mcp Snapshot OR migrate all browser automation to CDP-based driver pattern

errors_observed: windows-mcp Snapshot 'No windows found' entire session
DevTools Network row click landed in filter bar / Elements tab instead of intended row
Response panel never opened -- response body not captured
notes: Cycle 95 mistral envelope v5 retry. PARTIAL with engine-layer progress (200 OK, no TRPCClientError, v5 fix worked). UI rendering remains the blocker. Subagent 12min exceeded 8min hard timeout due to test infra issue (Snapshot blind). Next iteration needs response body capture to determine if frontend validation drops a valid envelope (silent reject pattern like gemini3 B36) or envelope content is wrong shape.
